snippet.title: Interview with the Rt  Hon  Michael Manley ON OCC Part 3/4
snippet.description:
Personally Speaking with Donette Chin-Loy. Produced by Frame by Frame Productions and Donette Chin-Loy. 

The last full length interview with former, late Prime Minister of Jamaica, Rt. Hon. Michael Norman Manley ON OCC.

Michael Norman Manley ON OCC (10 December 1924 -- 6 March 1997) was a Jamaican politician who served as the fourth Prime Minister of Jamaica from 1972 to 1980 and from 1989 to 1992. Coming from a prosperous background, Manley was a democratic socialist. He is considered one of Jamaica's best Prime Ministers since independence, according to public opinion polls.

The second son of Jamaica's Premier Norman Washington Manley and Jamaican artist Edna Manley, Michael Manley was a charismatic figure who became the leader of the Jamaican People's National Party a few months before his father's death in 1969...

snippet.title: FAE  ELLINGTON'S INTERVIEW WITH FORMER PRIME MINISTER OF JAMAICA, MICHAEL MANLEY... PART 2 VIDEO
snippet.description:
This interview with the late Michael Manley, former Prime Minister of Jamaica was recorded in 1995... 28 years ago. It had its first and only airing on JBC Radio One, on the now defunct Jamaica Broadcasting Corporation. I have decided to publish the conversation in seven parts, beginning on Sunday, September 10 with the final part to be published on Saturday, September 16, which will be the anniversary of my 49th year in broadcast media.

In some parts you'll hear the voices of Mr. Manley's wife, Glynne and that of programme musician, Marjorie Whylie. 

28 years ago broadcast engineer was Peter Brown. 
Thanks to Dadrian HoSang, Sound Engineer at Creative Sounds.
Special thanks to Damion Mitchell.

Pictures were provided by -
(c) The Gleaner Company (Media) Limited 
Mrs. Glynne Manley
Miss Sarah Manley
Mr. Granville Valentine, National Workers' Union
Michael Manley Foundation

I am certain that this interview in its 7 parts will provide new information and insights, generate discussion and passionate debate, and perhaps it will trigger ideas for further, reading, research and investigation.

Thanks for listening. Please share and comment.

Walk good.

Fae

snippet.title: Truth to be Told: Michael Manley in Conversation by Glynne Manley
snippet.description:
Michael Manley was Jamaica's Prime Minister between 1972 and 1980 when his government attempted a series of decolonizing reforms. He was defeated in a violent election in 1980 and the Washington consensus was ushered in. He returned to power in 1989 taking a more moderate stance. Manley's leadership style and legacy is heatedly contested, but does it have any relevance for the present moment? Truth be Told comprises three years of candid interviews with Michael Manley conducted by his wife Glynne leading up to his death in 1997.  Now, in the context of a major shift in the balance of power in the hemisphere, leading scholars of the Caribbean, Canada, the US and Jamaica take up the book in relation to Manley's legacy. They do so through the lens of contemporary issues related to political leadership, the informal economy, gender, mining and extraction, global racism and inequality; and land and labour rights.

Join the conversation with:

Anthony Bogues, Asa Messer Professor of Humanities and Critical Theory, Professor of Africana Studies, and Director of the Center for the Study of Slavery and Justice, Brown University

Kevin Edmonds, Assistant Professor, Caribbean Studies, University of Toronto

Glynne Manley, Interviewer and Editor Truth be Told

Maziki Thame, Professor, Institute of Development Studies, University of the West Indies Mona, Jamaica.

Anna Zalik, Associate Professor, Political Economy of Development, Faculty of Environmental Urban Change at York University

snippet.title: PART 4 FAE A  ELLINGTON'S INTERVIEW WITH MICHAEL MANLEY VIDEO TRADE UNIONISM
snippet.description:
This interview with the late Michael Manley, former Prime Minister of Jamaica was recorded in 1995... 28 years ago. It had it first and only airing on JBC Radio One, on the now defunct Jamaica Broadcasting Corporation. 

I have decided to publish the conversation in seven parts, beginning on Sunday, September 10 with the final part to be published on Saturday, September 16, which will be the anniversary of my 49th year in broadcast media. 

In some parts you'll hear the voices of Mr. Manley's wife, Glynne and that of programme musician, Marjorie Whylie. 
28 years ago broadcast engineer was Peter Brown. 

Thanks to Dadrian HoSang, Sound Engineer at Creative Sounds. 

Special thanks to Damion Mitchell. 

Pictures were provided by - (c) The Gleaner Company (Media) Limited 
Mrs. Glynne Manley 
Miss Sarah Manley 
Mr. Granville Valentine, National Workers' Union 
Michael Manley Foundation 

I am certain that this interview in its 7 parts will provide new information and insights, generate discussion and passionate debate, and perhaps it will trigger ideas for further, reading, research and investigation. 

Thanks for listening. Please share and comment. 

Walk good. 
Fae

snippet.title: Jamaica Black History | US Intervention and Regime Change in the Caribbean - Part 1 | Michael Manley
snippet.description:
In the 1970s, the US government, through the CIA, led a sustained campaign to topple the Manley administration and disrupt his democratic socialist agenda.

SOURCES:
- Killing Hope: US military and CIA interventions since World War 2 by William Blum
- Dependency and Socialism in the Modern Caribbean  : Superpower Intervention in Guyana, Jamaica, and Grenada by Euclid A. Rose

snippet.title: PART 7 FINAL PART   FAE A  ELLINGTON'S INTERVIEW WITH MICHAEL MANLEY
snippet.description:
This interview with the late Michael Manley, former Prime Minister of Jamaica was recorded in 1995... 28 years ago. It had it first and only airing on JBC Radio One, on the now defunct Jamaica Broadcasting Corporation. 

I have decided to publish the conversation in seven parts, beginning on Sunday, September 10 with the final part to be published on Saturday, September 16, which will be the anniversary of my 49th year in broadcast media. In some parts you'll hear the voices of Mr. Manley's wife, Glynne and that of programme musician, Marjorie Whylie. 

28 years ago broadcast engineer was Peter Brown. 

Thanks to Dadrian HoSang, Sound Engineer at Creative Sounds. 

Special thanks to Damion Mitchell. 

Pictures were provided by - (c) The Gleaner Company (Media) Limited 
Mrs. Glynne Manley 
Miss Sarah Manley 
Mr. Granville Valentine, National Workers' Union 
Michael Manley Foundation 

I am certain that this interview in its 7 parts will provide new information and insights, generate discussion and passionate debate, and perhaps it will trigger ideas for further, reading, research and investigation. Thanks for listening. Please share and comment. Walk good. Fae

snippet.title: Dreams of Michael Manley | Is The PNP Really Different From the JLP?
